resources.string: Detect a number on the screen
Compare a number displayed on your screen to a pre-defined value
Click only when the value is over 9000!

Welcome to the number condition tutorial!

Here, you will
        try to beat the high score of a game with the help of Klick'r.

You can first try it by pressing start game,
        or proceed to the Scenario configuration directly.
Just like any other Screen Condition, we need to create a Screen Event first.
Now, click on the Conditions field to open the Condition list.
We need to create the Condition. Click on Create.
In this tutorial, we want to learn how to detect a number on the screen, so select Number Condition
A Number Condition is defined by the number to detect, and a comparison to apply to it.

In this tutorial, we will only compare the detected number to a static value, but is possible to compare it to the value of a counter. Refers to the counters specific tutorials if you need for information about this feature.
We want to detect if the displayed value is over 9000. So first, click on operator to change the comparison operator.
Select the > operator.
Now, you need to enter the value to compare with. Simply enter \"9000\" in the value field to proceed to the next tutorial step.
Now, we need to tell Klick\'r where to search for the number.\n\nClick on the detection area selector to define it
Position and resize the detection rectangle so it covers the displayed number. You can start the game the see where it is shown. Once the area is correct, press the validate button to save it.
Try to use the smaller possible box while keeping a margin around the number to detect.

Your Condition is now configured!

There are now several adjustments that can be made to it, but the default settings suits our basic use case.

Click on the save button to register it and return to the Conditions list.
Close the Conditions list to return to the Event configuration.

With this Condition, the scenario knows when to trigger the Actions.

 You now need to define what Actions to trigger, so for this game, a click on the button.

Click on the Actions field to display the Actions list.

An Action is something that is executed once the Conditions of its Event are fulfilled.

It can be a click, a swipe, a pause, an Intent (an interaction with another application) or a change in the Scenario execution, and multiple Actions can be defined for the same Event.

Click on the create Action button to create your first Action.
First, we need to pick a type for your Action.\n\nFor this game, we simply need to click on the button, so choose Click to create a new Click Action.

A Click Action is defined by a position and a press duration.

As we want to click as quickly as possible, we can keep the default press duration of 1ms, which is the lowest possible value. So we only need to define a position.

Click on the position picker button to select the Click Action position.

You need to select the exact position to be clicked, so you need to start the game again to display the button, and then select its position.\n\nOnce the position is correct, click on the validate button to save it.
You can\'t interact with the game while the position selector is visible, but you can use this button to show and hide it, allowing you to remove that restriction at will.
The click is now correctly configured!\n\nClick on the save button to register it and return to the Actions list.
Close the Actions list to return to the Event configuration.
Your Event now has a Condition and an Action, which means it knows what to execute and when to execute it.\n\nClick on the save button to register it and return to the Scenario dialog.

The only requirement for a Scenario is to have at least one Event.
 As our Event already cover how to win the game, we don't need anything else.

Click on the save button to save the Scenario in database and close the configuration dialogs.
As you can see, the play button is now available, meaning that your Scenario is valid and can be used for detection.\n\nClick on it to start the detection.

It\'s playing but nothing happens?\n\nThat\'s normal, it will click only when the button displays a number over 9000, remember? While keeping the scenario running, press the start game button and just watch as Klick\'r will beat it for you!

Congratulations!

You now know how to detect a number displayed on the screen.
Take a look at the other tutorials to discover more use cases.
